title: xyris terrestris
intro: Xyris terrestris, also known as Yellow-eyed Grass, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Xyridaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas, and its rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Xyris terrestris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
